mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production signal broad economic expansion and improving corporate earnings outlooks. This positive fundamental momentum translates into positive equity returns as investors price in higher future growth.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a coincident indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between 3-month changes in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.03 over any rolling 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals a sharp deterioration in the labor market, which is a strong leading indicator of recession. This reduces expected consumer demand and corporate profits, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following the trigger condition being met during a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: High and accelerating inflation, when not met with a corresponding rise in the federal funds rate, erodes real corporate earnings and signals a central bank 'behind the curve', increasing risk premia and causing negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year CPIAUCSL change > 4% AND 3-month change in FEDFUNDS < 0.10pp
failure_condition: The combined signal does not have a statistically significant negative correlation with 60-day forward returns (p > 0.05) in a full-sample backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation environment, exceptionally strong retail sales growth signals overheating demand, increasing the probability of aggressive monetary tightening by the central bank, which negatively impacts equity valuations through a higher discount rate.
failure_condition: In periods where year-over-year CPI is above 3%, the correlation between 3-month RSAFS growth and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive or statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Strong growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects a robust and expanding economy. This directly translates to higher aggregate income and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive equity market performance over the very short term.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS is a key component of NBER recession dating.
failure_condition: The 1-month change in PAYEMS shows a rank information coefficient less than 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window against 5-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in housing starts indicates weakening consumer confidence and construction activity, acting as a leading indicator for a broader economic slowdown and negatively affecting equity markets.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a highly interest-rate sensitive and cyclical sector.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances across a full backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of decelerating industrial production and accelerating inflation creates a stagflationary environment. This dynamic compresses corporate profit margins and increases the equity risk premium, leading to negative returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in INDPRO < 0 AND 3-month change in CPIAUCSL > 0.5%
failure_condition: A strategy of shorting the S&P 500 when the trigger condition is met fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle. This compresses the discount rates applied to equity cash flows and signals central bank support for the economy, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return following a month where FEDFUNDS declined is not statistically significantly greater than the average return in all other months (t-test p > 0.05).
